### Yesterdays To do!

- [x] start github
- [x] initialize Julia environment for the project
- [ ] investigation of 'How to implement?' and 'How to visiualize?'
- [ ] simple implementation of various and visualization 

---

### getting started: Hénon Heils Model

**Comments for the day:** 

I first worked on some further workflow questions. Including a bash script for adding entries from the 'glossary.json' to the 'glossary.qmd' file and to the '_quarto.qmd' file, for latex macros or to initiate the day-log by the use of templates.

I use the ai-tool provided by claude to help generate the bash-scripts for workflow and to communicate about the structure of my workspace. I have general idea from previous projects how I want to structure my workspace - though I do not have the knowledge in coding in 'bash' or 'yml' etc... 

So I use ai to set up my folder structure and to prepare the workflow. I also need to keep track of my ai usage for transparency.

---

#### suggested structure

1) derivation
2) considering derivation
2) numerical implementation

---

#### observations 

- [original paper by Henon and Heiles](https://articles.adsabs.harvard.edu/pdf/1964AJ.....69...73H) @Henon1964
  - I came across, need to undertand: 6-D phase space -> 5 independant conservative integrals of the motion; seemingly, sure but I want a rigorous understanding
  - results in 5 hyper surfaces, **their intersections define the trajectories**

---

- literature by @strogatz1994 under chapter 12 strange attractors
- [wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/H%C3%A9non_map)
  - 'orbit diagram' or 'bifracation diagram'
  - **is the Héno Map the same as the Hénon–Heiles model?** I dont think so!

---

### Lost traveler thoughts

- connections of strange attractor to Cantor Set @Arnold1989 p. 430-431;
- gets me to think about how ternary number representation (base 3) is a useful way to analyze the Cantor set

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![How the manningfold gets created.](/pic/26-05-27-03-strangeAttractors.png)

![The relation to the cantor set.](/pic/26-05-27-01-strangeAttractors.png)
:::
